description: Artisans, smiths, and skilled makers of goods.
jobs: Blacksmith
A soot-streaked figure hammering red-hot iron into horseshoes, hinges, and blades.
Weaponsmith
Sparks flying as a muscled smith folds, tempers, and grinds steel into swords and spearheads.
Armorer
A rivet-scarred craftsman shaping steel plates into breastplates, helms, and greaves.
Goldsmith
A loupe-squinting artisan melting gold and setting gemstones into rings and coronets.
Silversmith
Polished hands hammering silver into goblets and fine tableware for noble houses.
Coppersmith
A green-patina-stained worker beating copper sheets into kettles, pots, and basins.
Tinsmith
Quick hands cutting and soldering thin tin into cups, lanterns, and cookware.
Locksmith
A file-worn specialist crafting intricate wards and pins that resist every picking attempt.
Carpenter
A sawdust-dusted joiner fitting timber into houses, furniture, and roof beams.
Woodcarver
A shaving-curled artisan gouging and chiseling raw wood into figures and reliefs.
Cooper
A hoop-driving craftsman bending oak staves into watertight barrels.
Wheelwright
A spoke-shaving builder of sturdy wheels for carts and wagons.
Cartwright
A complete-vehicle builder constructing carts and wagons from axle to tailgate.
Shipwright
A tar-smelling builder laying keels, steaming planks, and caulking seams for seaworthy vessels.
Mason
A mortar-crusted layer of stone raising walls, arches, and foundations.
Stonemason
A chisel-calloused cutter dressing raw quarry blocks into precise stones for cathedrals.
Sculptor
A marble-dusted carver shaping granite into statues, gargoyles, and ornamental friezes.
Bricklayer
A level-eyed stacker of fired clay bricks building walls and chimneys in even courses.
Thatcher
A ladder-perched roofer bundling reeds into thick layers that shed rain from cottage roofs.
Roofer
A height-braving worker laying slate and tile to keep water out of buildings.
Glazier
A lead-fingered cutter fitting glass panes into windows, sometimes with colored designs.
Potter
Clay-caked hands throwing wet earth on a spinning wheel, firing it into bowls and jugs.
Weaver
A shuttle-passing worker interlacing warp and weft on a clacking loom into bolts of cloth.
Spinner
A wheel-turning worker twisting raw wool or flax into thread ready for the loom.
Dyer
A vat-stained worker soaking cloth in indigo, madder, and weld to set vivid colors.
Tailor
A tape-draped cutter measuring, stitching, and fitting fabric into coats and gowns.
Seamstress
A needle-pricked mender sewing seams, hemming dresses, and patching torn garments.
Cobbler
A leather-aproned patcher replacing worn soles and heels to keep old boots on the road.
Cordwainer
A last-and-awl craftsman stitching fine new shoes and boots from soft leather by hand.
Tanner
A sharp-smelling worker with cracked hands, soaking hides in bark and lime to make leather.
Leatherworker
A stamp-and-stitch specialist cutting cured leather into belts, pouches, and saddlebags.
Furrier
A pelt-stretching artisan cleaning and sewing animal furs into cloaks, linings, and trim.
Chandler
A wax-splattered dipper of wicks producing candles by the dozen from tallow and beeswax.
Soap Maker
A lye-burned boiler of rendered fat, producing blocks of cleaning soap in steaming vats.
Perfumer
A flower-stained distiller of resins and spices into bottled fragrances and scented oils.
Glassblower
A heat-flushed artisan gathering molten glass on a pipe and blowing it into vases and beads.
Jeweler
A gem-setting perfectionist mounting precious stones into gold and silver rings and pendants.
Clockmaker
A magnifying-glass-wearing assembler of tiny gears and springs into ticking timepieces.
Instrument Maker
A tone-testing carver of lutes, flutes, and harps from fine wood and gut string.
Fletcher
A feather-gluing maker of arrows, splitting shafts and fitting iron points by the score.
Saddler
A wax-thread-pulling stitcher of thick leather over wooden trees for horse and rider.
Rope Maker
A hemp-twisting walker spinning fibers into coils of sturdy rope on a long ropewalk.
Net Maker
A twine-knotting craftsman tying mesh for fishing seines, cast nets, and game traps.
Basket Weaver
A willow-bending worker shaping withies into baskets, panniers, and wicker creels.
Broom Maker
A twig-binding maker fastening birch or broomcorn to wooden handles for sweeping.
Brewer
A mash-stirring ale-maker boiling hops and fermenting wort into foaming casks.
Apprentice
A wide-eyed youth sweeping the shop, fetching tools, and learning a trade under a master's eye.
Journeyman
A traveling craftsman selling skilled labor between shops until earning a master's title.
Master Craftsman
A guild-standard setter running a workshop and training the next generation of apprentices.
Carver
A blade-wielding servant slicing roasts at the lord's table with practiced, elegant strokes.
Engraver
A burin-gripping artist cutting letters, crests, and fine designs into metal and stone.
Calligrapher
A quill-flourishing scribe writing charters in flowing ornamental script.
Engineer
A compass-wielding designer of bridges, aqueducts, and fortifications built on geometry.
Master Builder
A site-commanding director of masons, carpenters, and laborers on major construction.
Runesmith
A chisel-scarred inscriber binding arcane symbols into weapons and armor.
Tinker
A pot-clinking wanderer mending kettles, sharpening knives, and patching cookware on the road.
Knife Grinder
A wheel-spinning sharpener pressing dull blades against stone until they shave clean.
Tallow Maker
A grease-slick renderer of animal fat, pouring it hot into candle molds.
Salt Maker
A wind-chapped evaporator of seawater in shallow pans, harvesting crystals of salt.
Ship Builder
A seaside-yard worker constructing hulls from keel to gunwale in salt-stiffened clothes.
Bridge Builder
A piling-sinking builder laying timbers to span rivers and gorges.
Road Builder
A gravel-hauling crew boss grading earth and setting paving stones through wild country.
Smelter
A furnace-feeding worker extracting molten metal from ore and charcoal in roaring heat.
Charcoal Maker
A smoke-blackened tender stacking wood in earthen mounds and slow-burning it for forges.
Glassmaker
A furnace-tending melter of sand, ash, and lime into raw glass for shaping.
Mirror Maker
A silver-coating finisher polishing metal or glass into clear reflective surfaces.
Lens Grinder
A patience-worn polisher shaping curved glass into lenses for spectacles and spyglasses.
Clock Maker
A counterweight-fitting mechanic assembling cogs into tower clocks and mantelpiece mechanisms.
Toy Maker
A paint-spotted carver of wooden horses and tin soldiers for children's delight.
Doll Maker
A tiny-stitch sewer sculpting faces from clay and dressing small cloth bodies.
Mask Maker
A papier-mache-molding artist crafting festival masks both comic and terrifying.
Costume Maker
A dye-stained sewer of elaborate costumes with padding and flourishes for stage and festival.
Banner Maker
A silk-cutting artisan stitching and dyeing heraldic banners and pennants.
Poison Maker
A glove-wearing extractor of toxins from plants, fungi, and serpents into lethal vials.
Antidote Maker
A careful blender of herbs and minerals into remedies that neutralize known poisons.
Love Potion Maker
A rose-petal-crushing brewer of tinctures rumored to kindle infatuation and desire.
Curse Maker
A wax-dripping binder of malice into effigies and talismans that bring misfortune.
Charm Maker
A knot-tying inscriber of small tokens meant to carry luck and protection.
Amulet Crafter
A gem-setting enchanter mounting charmed stones into pendants that ward off evil.
Treasure Map Maker
A riddle-marking cartographer drawing coded parchments leading to hidden caches.
Deal Maker
A handshake-sealing broker of agreements between merchants, guilds, and nobles for a cut.
Embroiderer
A silk-threaded artist stitching flowers, crests, and scenes into fine fabric.
Lace Maker
A bobbin-twisting artisan weaving dozens of threads into delicate patterns of lace on a pillow.
Ribbon Maker
A small-loom weaver producing narrow bands of silk and satin ribbon.
Button Maker
A bone-and-horn turner shaping small fasteners for coats and doublets.
Pin Cushion Maker
A sawdust-stuffing maker of small fabric pads for holding pins and needles.
Thimble Maker
A tiny-cap stamper forming metal finger guards that protect seamstresses from needle pricks.
Needle Maker
A wire-drawing specialist punching eyes and sharpening points into sewing needles.
Thread Spinner
A cotton-twisting worker spinning fine, even thread for stitching and embroidery.
Yarn Maker
A dye-stained plier of wool fibers into skeins of yarn for knitting and weaving.
Rope Spinner
A ropewalk-pacing twister of long fiber strands into heavy-duty coils.
Twine Maker
A hemp-spinning maker of light cord for tying parcels, bundles, and garden stakes.
String Maker
A gut-and-silk producer of fine, strong strings for instruments, bows, and lacing.
Cord Maker
A braid-fingered worker producing medium-weight cord for drawstrings and rigging.
Cable Maker
A hawser-laying specialist twisting multiple rope strands into thick cables for ships.
Chain Maker
A link-welding smith bending iron rods into chains for anchors and drawbridges.
Nail Maker
A forge-side hammerer cutting iron rods and pointing each into a nail with quick blows.
Screw Maker
A thread-filing specialist carving spiral grooves into metal shafts for presses and joinery.
Bolt Maker
A heavy-duty forger of bolts and pins that hold timber frames and iron gates together.
Rivet Maker
A heading-hammer worker shaping iron pins for permanently fastening armor and ship hulls.
Hinge Maker
A pivot-forging smith crafting joints that let doors, chests, and shutters swing open.
Lock Maker
A tumbler-assembling security craftsman fitting spring-loaded wards inside iron casings.
Key Maker
A blank-filing cutter shaping brass and iron into keys matched to specific locks.
Safe Maker
A strongbox-building specialist constructing iron-walled vaults with hidden lock mechanisms.
Strongbox Maker
A plate-riveting builder reinforcing iron around a wooden core for secure storage.
Chest Maker
A plank-joining carpenter fitting lids and mounting iron bands on storage chests.
Trunk Maker
A leather-covering builder of reinforced wooden trunks designed for long-distance travel.
Box Maker
A fine-joinery craftsman building small wooden boxes for jewelry, spices, or writing supplies.
Crate Maker
A rough-board nailer building open-framed crates for shipping heavy cargo.
Barrel Maker
An oak-stave steamer bending and hooping barrels for ale, wine, and salted fish.
Keg Maker
A tight-seal-testing cooper building small kegs sized for tavern taps and traveling.
Cask Maker
A char-firing cooper building medium casks to age wine, brandy, and whiskey.
Tub Maker
A wide-stave shaper of open wooden tubs used for washing, dyeing, and brewing.
Bucket Maker
A hoop-driving maker of handled wooden buckets for hauling water from the well.
Pail Maker
A stave-fitting builder of small wooden pails for milking, berries, and fetching water.
Pot Maker
A kiln-tending potter firing clay into thick-walled pots for stews over the hearth.
Pan Maker
A copper-hammering smith shaping flat, handled pans for frying and baking.
Kettle Maker
A spout-fitting metalworker shaping kettles that hang above the fire for boiling.
Cauldron Maker
A mold-casting founder of heavy iron cauldrons large enough to feed a garrison.
Brazier Maker
A metal-shaping smith hammering portable fire bowls used for warmth and cooking.
Lantern Maker
A glass-and-tin fitter assembling windproof frames to shield candle flames.
Candle Maker
A wick-dipping worker molding wax and tallow into tapers that light homes and chapels.
Torch Maker
A pitch-rag wrapper binding fuel-soaked cloth around wooden shafts for reliable, smoky light.
Oil Lamp Maker
A reservoir-shaping craftsman of clay and metal lamps with wicks that burn oil for light.
Wick Maker
A cotton-braiding worker twisting fibers into wicks that draw fuel steadily to a flame.
Tinder Maker
A char-cloth preparer of dry tinder bundles for striking fire in any weather.
Flint Knapper
A stone-chipping specialist flaking flint into sharp-edged strikers and arrowheads.
Fire Steel Maker
A carbon-steel forger of small strikers that throw hot sparks against flint.
Forge Tender
A coal-feeding helper keeping the forge temperature steady for the smith's work.
Bellows Operator
A leather-pumping laborer blasting air into the forge to raise its white-hot heat.
Anvil Striker
A sledge-swinging helper adding brute force wherever the blacksmith points.
Tool Maker
A hammer-forging specialist crafting the tongs, chisels, and files other craftsmen need.
Implement Maker
A farm-tool builder forging hoes, rakes, scythes, and plowshares for field work.
Utensil Maker
A spoon-carving shaper of ladles and serving forks from wood, bone, or pewter.
Cutlery Maker
A handle-fitting grinder of table knives, carving blades, and eating sets.
Knife Maker
A belt-knife forger grinding utility blades for kitchen, hunting pouch, and daily carry.
Blade Smith
A fold-and-temper specialist producing razor-keen edges for knives and short swords.
Sword Smith
A balance-testing forger of longswords, weighing flex, edge, and heft in every blade.
Dagger Smith
A slim-blade craftsman forging double-edged daggers and stilettos for close work.
Spear Maker
An ash-shaft fitter mounting forged iron points for infantry and hunting spears.
Pike Maker
A polearm-lengthening builder tipping extra-long shafts with steel for anti-cavalry work.
Halberd Maker
An axe-and-spike mounter fitting versatile heads onto stout polearm shafts.
Axe Maker
A haft-fitting forger of single- and double-bitted axes for war and woodcutting.
Mace Maker
A flanged-head caster mounting heavy spiked steel onto wooden hafts to crush armor.
Hammer Maker
A steel-head forger fitting oak handles for war hammers and devastating mauls.
Flail Maker
A chain-linking builder connecting spiked iron balls to wooden handles for crushing swings.
Club Maker
A hardwood shaper carving weighted clubs and cudgels, sometimes studded with iron.
Staff Maker
A wood-seasoning selector shaping sturdy hardwood into walking and fighting staves.
Wand Maker
A rare-wood carver coring slender wands with magical materials for arcane focus.
Rod Maker
An ornamental turner of metal and wood into rods of office and arcane implements.
Scepter Maker
A gem-topping goldsmith crafting ceremonial scepters of precious metal for royalty.
Crown Maker
A filigree-setting forger of circlets and crowns fitted with jewels for coronation.
Tiara Maker
A fine-wire bender setting small gems into delicate tiaras for noblewomen.
Diadem Maker
A band-fashioning metalsmith crafting simple marks of rank and authority.
Circlet Maker
A thin-metal hammerer shaping elegant headbands worn beneath hoods and helms.
Helm Maker
A visor-fitting shaper of steel helms with nasals and aventails for head protection.
Helmet Smith
A dome-stake raiser lifting a single sheet of steel into a rounded battle helm.
Visor Maker
A hinge-and-slot specialist crafting face-plates that lift and lock on knightly helms.
Shield Maker
A boss-riveting builder layering wood, leather, and iron into shields that turn blows.
Buckler Maker
A fist-shield forger of small, round parrying shields for close combat.
Targe Maker
A leather-stretching maker of round wooden highland shields with central grips.
Pavise Maker
A tall-shield builder constructing freestanding covers that protect crossbowmen while reloading.
Mail Maker
A ring-riveting worker winding, cutting, and linking thousands of tiny iron rings into chain mail.
Scale Maker
A punch-and-lace worker overlapping small metal scales onto leather backing in rows.
Plate Maker
A steel-articulating armorer hammering full suits of plate, joint by fitted joint.
Gauntlet Maker
A tiny-plate riveter articulating flexible steel hand armor for mounted knights.
Sabaton Maker
A lame-riveting smith crafting pointed steel boots for armored cavalry.
Greave Maker
A shin-plate shaper curving steel guards that strap around the lower legs.
Cuirass Maker
A torso-guarding smith forming front-and-back steel plates for battle protection.
Brigandine Maker
A canvas-and-steel riveter sandwiching small plates between fabric layers for a flexible vest.
Gambeson Maker
A linen-quilting stitcher layering padded jackets worn under or instead of armor.
Leather Armor Maker
A boil-and-shape specialist hardening leather into cuirasses, bracers, and greaves.
Studded Armor Maker
An iron-stud driver reinforcing leather armor against blows with metal points.
Banded Armor Maker
A strip-riveting worker fastening horizontal iron bands across leather to stiffen it.
Splint Armor Maker
A splint-lacing builder tying vertical metal strips to leather for flexible limb defense.
Ring Armor Maker
A large-ring sewer stitching metal circles onto cloth garments for basic protection.
Lamellar Armor Maker
A plate-lacing artisan binding hundreds of small rectangles into a rigid armored vest.
Scale Armor Maker
A fish-skin-pattern tier overlapping metal scales onto a fabric shirt.
Chain Armor Maker
A wire-drawing interlocker of rings into shirts, coifs, and leggings of chain.
Mesh Armor Maker
A fine-wire weaver of tight metal mesh worn under clothing for concealed protection.
Padded Armor Maker
A thick-fabric stuffer quilting jackets that absorb blunt impacts beneath plate.
Quilted Armor Maker
A diamond-pattern stitcher layering multiple cloth sheets into blow-cushioning jackets.
Reinforced Clothing Maker
A hidden-plate tailor concealing metal and chain patches inside ordinary-looking garments.
Battle Garb Maker
A quick-release-clasp sewer of war-ready clothing with concealed padding underneath.
War Paint Mixer
A pigment-grinding mixer of bold face and body paints worn into battle.
Battle Standard Maker
A weather-proofing sewer of large regimental flags built to survive wind, rain, and war.
Regimental Flag Maker
A silk-embroidering stitcher of unit insignia and battle honors onto company colors.
National Flag Maker
A kingdom's-colors cutter stitching flags flown from towers and ship masts.
Ceremonial Flag Maker
A gold-thread embroiderer of richly decorated banners for coronations and holy rites.
